target.title: fix: repair historical ClawPack release files
target.body:
Fixes #1991

## Summary
- repair historical npm-pack ClawPack releases whose stored release file rows are shorter than the recorded artifact file count
- move the historical row rewrite behind explicit operator migration actions instead of ordinary package scan backfill
- add dry-run/default-safe output, cursor/resume progress, package/version/release targeting, destructive-write confirmation, and operator-visible validation logs
- fix targeted migration lookup so `releaseId`, `packageName`, and `packageName+version` paths use indexes/direct lookups instead of filtering after the first global active-release page
- parse the stored artifact, persist implementation entries, recompute the release fingerprint, reset stale package review state, and queue fresh static/Codex package scans after confirmed repair
- update the security moderation spec so ClawPack inspect/review surfaces use artifact implementation files and document the repair migration contract

## Operator migration contract
Dry run first:

```bash
bunx convex run packages:repairHistoricalClawPackReleaseFiles '{"dryRun":true,"batchSize":25,"maxBatches":1}'
```

Target narrowly before an all-release pass:

```bash
bunx convex run packages:repairHistoricalClawPackReleaseFiles '{"dryRun":true,"packageName":"@openclaw/codex-claw","version":"2026.5.6","batchSize":10}'
```

Apply requires explicit confirmation:

```bash
bunx convex run packages:repairHistoricalClawPackReleaseFiles '{"dryRun":false,"confirmation":"repair-historical-clawpack-files","packageName":"@openclaw/codex-claw","version":"2026.5.6","batchSize":10}'
```

Resume with the returned `cursor`; use `scheduleNext:true` only when intentionally enqueueing the next batch.

## P1 review fix
The migration batch query now resolves targets before scanning release pages:
- `releaseId`: direct release lookup
- `packageName+version`: `packages.by_name` then `packageReleases.by_package_version`
- `packageName`: `packages.by_name` then paginated `packageReleases.by_package_active_created`
- no target: global `packageReleases.by_active_created` cursor scan

Regression coverage includes a matching `packageName+version` release beyond the first global page and asserts the global paginator is not touched.

## Behavior proof
Local handler dry-run proof with redacted IDs. This exercises the actual `repairHistoricalClawPackReleaseFilesInternal` action handler against a local ClawPack `.tgz` fixture and mocked Convex context; it does not require writes and verifies no storage/mutation/scheduler calls happen in dry-run mode.

```console
$ bun --eval '<local ClawPack fixture invokes repairHistoricalClawPackReleaseFilesInternal._handler>'
{
  "stats": {
    "scanned": 1,
    "candidates": 1,
    "validated": 1,
    "wouldRepair": 1,
    "repaired": 0,
    "skipped": 0,
    "errors": 0
  },
  "cursor": null,
  "isDone": true,
  "samples": [
    {
      "releaseId": "packageReleases:<redacted>",
      "packageId": "packages:<redacted>",
      "packageName": "@scope/demo",
      "version": "1.0.0",
      "existingFileCount": 2,
      "npmFileCount": 4,
      "repairedFiles": 4,
      "integritySha256": "b56baa50842cbbc3efc37ac21d82022dbc46b22a7f9f63abd03b1d3b34086490",
      "artifactSha256": "4ce7b612200be722db848059272e127c4eed47738d888a0b2db0cb99f20d0948",
      "samplePaths": ["package.json", "openclaw.plugin.json", "README.md", "dist/index.js"]
    }
  ],
  "logLines": [
    "[clawpack-repair] mode=dry-run batchSize=1 maxBatches=1",
    "[clawpack-repair] batch=1 scanned=1 candidates=1 nextCursor=<done>",
    "[clawpack-repair] would-repair package=@scope/demo version=1.0.0 release=packageReleases:<redacted> files=2->4 npmFileCount=4 integrity=b56baa50842c..."
  ]
}
```

Real Convex/local deployment proof is still blocked in this fresh checkout because there is no configured Convex deployment and this environment cannot create a local deployment while anonymous:

```console
$ bunx convex codegen
✖ No CONVEX_DEPLOYMENT set, run `npx convex dev` to configure a Convex project

$ bunx convex deployment select local && bunx convex dev --once --typecheck=disable --codegen=disable
✖ No local deployment found. Run npx convex deployment create local to create one.

$ bunx convex deployment create local
✖ Cannot create a deployment in anonymous mode. Run `npx convex login` and configure a project first.
```

## Validation
- `bun run test convex/packages.public.test.ts --testNamePattern "ClawPack|package scan backfill" --reporter=dot` passed: 22 tests passed, 191 skipped
- `bun run format:check -- convex/packages.ts convex/packages.public.test.ts specs/security-moderation.md` passed
- `bunx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` passed
- `git diff --check` passed
- `bun run ci:static` passed
- `bun run ci:unit` passed after clearing ignored generated `coverage/`: 256 files passed, 1 skipped; 3039 tests passed, 1 skipped; coverage thresholds passed. Two prior `ci:unit` attempts failed in Vitest coverage report generation with `ENOENT coverage/.tmp/coverage-*.json`; no test assertion failed.
- `bun run ci:types-build` passed; existing Shiki wasm fallback warning appeared during build but exited cleanly

## Notes
- `bunx convex codegen` and real local Convex runtime proof remain blocked by missing `CONVEX_DEPLOYMENT` / Convex login in this fresh checkout.
- Commit author range verified with `git log --format='%h %an <%ae> %s' upstream/main..HEAD`; all branch commits use `Andy Ye <35905412+TurboTheTurtle@users.noreply.github.com>`.
target.author: turbotheturtle
